data.description:
# Cluster 1 — consolidate canonical-JSON/content-hash onto the `_store/canonical.py` seam (byte-preserving)

## What

Make `src/rebar/_store/canonical.py` the single home for "sorted-key compact JSON (+ sha256)" and
migrate the four real duplicate sites onto it, **preserving every site's exact output bytes** (pure
DRY — see the parent epic `civil-marlin-flare` for the no-live-bug analysis).

- Extend `src/rebar/_store/canonical.py`:
  - `canonical_str(doc, *, ascii_only=False, default=None)` — keyword-only params whose defaults
    reproduce today's exact bytes (`ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")`); the
    6 existing event-serialization importers and the byte-parity contract test are unaffected.
  - `canonical_bytes(doc, *, ascii_only=False, default=None)` and
    `content_hash(doc, *, ascii_only=False, default=None) -> str` (= `sha256(canonical_bytes(...))`).
- Migrate each site to import the seam and delete its local reimplementation:
  - `src/rebar/signing.py` `_canonical_payload` — already uses the canonical kwargs → byte-identical.
  - `src/rebar/llm/workflow/schema.py` `canonical_json`/`content_hash` — keep the public names as
    thin re-exports/delegators (preserve `llm/workflow/__init__.py` `__all__`).
  - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/conflict_resolver.py` `_hash_value` — call with
    `default=str` (cited consumer: hashes provenance values that may be non-JSON-native).
  - `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/mutation.py` `serialize_manifest` — call with
    `ascii_only=True` (PRESERVE `ensure_ascii=True`; do NOT normalize — its manifest hash is benign
    and changing it is a gratuitous behavior change).

## Why

Five near-identical canonical encoders have already drifted on `ensure_ascii`; one tested seam
removes the duplication and the drift risk while changing zero output bytes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/rebar/_store/canonical.py` exposes `canonical_str`/`canonical_bytes` (with `ascii_only` +
      `default` keyword params) and `content_hash`; the one true encoding is documented in the module.
- [ ] `signing.py`, `llm/workflow/schema.py`, `conflict_resolver.py`, and `mutation.py` import the
      seam; their local `json.dumps(...)`/`hashlib.sha256(...)` reimplementations are deleted.
- [ ] Every retained variant is an explicit, justified param with a cited consumer: `ascii_only=True`
      (`mutation.py`), `default=str` (`conflict_resolver.py`).
- [ ] `schema.py`'s public `canonical_json` / `content_hash` names still resolve (re-export preserved).
- [ ] A characterization test in `tests/unit/` asserts every migrated caller's output bytes are
      **unchanged** vs a frozen pre-refactor snapshot for inputs incl. non-ASCII, nested, and
      unicode-key payloads; and a signing sign→verify round-trip is unchanged.
- [ ] `make lint` and `make test` pass; `canonical.py` stays well under the 800-LOC cap; no new module.
data.title: Cluster 1: consolidate canonical-JSON/content-hash onto the _store/canonical.py seam (byte-preserving)
